decision_path.text: Troubleshoot Relay/Short- circuited wiring. Repair as needed
Troubleshoot Power Supply [PWS100]/open wiring. Repair as needed

decision_path.text: Check Ventilation and Fans Let power supply cool down.
Troubleshoot Short-circuited wiring. Repair as needed
Troubleshoot 110 VAC supply circuit (Check outlet to the SICK cabinet). Repair as needed
Switch Circuit Breaker/Main Switch [F11,F12,F13] to ON
Troubleshoot Power Supply/Short- circuited wiring. Repair as needed
Troubleshoot Short-circuited wiring. Repair as needed
Troubleshoot open wiring. Repair as needed
Troubleshoot open wiring to Camera/Illumilator. Repair as needed

text: Start by checking the Power Supply LED [G1,G2,G3]; if the LED is on, proceed to inspect the Circuit Breaker L[F111,F113,F115], R[F121,F123,F125], B[F131,F133,F135], and if any of these are off, switch the affected circuit breaker to on, then verify if it remains on, and if it does not stay on, troubleshoot short-circuited wiring and repair as needed, then repeat the functionality test; if the LED is red, determine if it is flashing, and if it is, check ventilation and fans to let the power supply cool down, otherwise troubleshoot short-circuited wiring and repair as needed; if the LED is off, proceed to check the Circuit Breaker/Main Switch [F11,F12,F13], ensure it is on, then verify if the switch remains on, and if it does not stay on, troubleshoot the 110 VAC supply circuit (check outlet to the SICK cabinet) and repair as needed, then repeat the functionality test; if the LED is on and the circuit breakers are also on, troubleshoot open wiring and repair as needed.

decision_path.text: Power Cycle and verify power. Replace if continues
Power cycle and watch illuminator during power up.
Verify Power connection and repeat test. Replace if still unresponsive.
Does light come on when system is triggered?
Verify illumination connection and repeat. Replace camera if light still stays on.
Does Result light flash when valid barcode facing camera? No
Power cycle and repeat test. Have programming verified if continues

text: Start by checking if the Device Ready Light is on; if it is green, proceed to verify if the service light is illuminated. If the service light is illuminated, replace the camera. If the service light is not illuminated, power cycle the device while observing the illuminator during power-up. Next, assess what the illuminator does; if it remains lit, verify the illumination connection and repeat the process, replacing the camera if the light continues to stay on. If the illuminator does not come on during activation, verify and correct the CAN wiring before retesting. If the light comes on, check whether the result light flashes when a valid barcode faces the camera; if it does not, power cycle the system and repeat the test, verifying network connections if the issue persists. If the Device Ready Light is not on, power cycle the device and observe the illuminator during startup. Next, determine what the illuminator does; if there is no activity, verify the power connection and repeat the test. If the illuminator remains unresponsive, replace the device.

decision_path.text: Check All CAN connection on all cameras and VMS. Connect cable or replace cable as needed
Replace the terminator for X8 - CAN

text: Begin by examining the X8 – CAN "Act" LED; if it flickers during operation, recheck the operation. If the LED is off, examine the 4 CAN wires and check if they are attached; if not, attach the wires and test again. If the wires are attached, check all CAN connections on all cameras and VMS, connecting or replacing cables as needed. If the system is working correctly, resume operation; otherwise, replace the terminator for X8 – CAN. If the issue persists after attaching the wires or addressing connections, proceed to replace the terminator.

decision_path.text: CAN – Device is missing. Check CAN Connection/Device
Device is busy. Application (SE & CE) will not run properly. Reboot system.
Yes Do both “Encoder Input” LEDs remain constantly on when the conveyor belt is running? X5
Cycle power to SIM Controller. If issue persists, replace Controller
Check the physical Ethernet connection on both ends of the cables’s connection points
Cameras light up when package enters tunnel

text: Begin by checking the "Power" LED X2; if it is off, verify the CAN connection for a missing device or cycle the power to the SIM Controller, and if the issue persists, replace the Controller; if the Power LED X2 is on, proceed to the "Device Ready" LED, and if this LED is Green, advance to the "System Ready" LED, then to the "Function 1" LED; if the "Function 1" LED is White, navigate to the "Trigger Input" LED X4 Input A, otherwise, if the "Power" LED X2 is off, proceed to "Trigger Input TS"; if the condition is "ON/OFF for every Package," address the question "Do both 'Encoder Input' LEDs remain constantly on when the conveyor belt is running?" and if the answer is no, proceed to "Encoder Input TS"; simultaneously, start at the "CAN" LED X8-CAN, and if it is off, proceed to "CAN TS"; if the CAN LED flickers during operation, check the "ETHERNET 1-4" LEDs X9–X12, and if any indicate an issue, verify the physical Ethernet connection; if all Ethernet LEDs are green, confirm that cameras light up when a package enters the tunnel, and if functionality is confirmed, repeat the System Functionality Test.

decision_path.text: Check Ventilation and Fans Let power supply cool down.
Switch Circuit Breaker/Camera Switch [CB10] to ON
Troubleshoot Relay/Short- circuited wiring. Repair as needed
Troubleshoot 110 VAC supply circuit (Check outlet to the SICK cabinet). Repair as needed
Switch Circuit Breaker/Main Switch [CB1] to ON
Troubleshoot Power Supply/Short- circuited wiring. Repair as needed
Troubleshoot Short-circuited wiring. Repair as needed
Troubleshoot open wiring to SIM2000. Repair as needed

text: Check the Power Supply LED status; if it is on, verify its color. If the LED is red, determine if it is flashing; if flashing, check ventilation and fans to cool the power supply. If the LED is green, proceed to Circuit Breaker/Camera Switch [CB10]. If the Power Supply LED is off, switch Circuit Breaker/Camera Switch [CB10] to ON and confirm if [CB10] remains ON; if not, troubleshoot relay or short-circuited wiring and repair as needed. If [CB10] remains ON, check if Fuse [FU122] is intact; if not, repair open wiring to SIM2000. If the Power Supply LED is off, navigate to Circuit Breaker/Main Switch [CB1], switch [CB1] to ON, and verify if [CB1] remains ON; if not, troubleshoot the Power Supply or short-circuited wiring. If [CB1] remains ON, check the 110 VAC supply circuit by examining the outlet to the SICK cabinet and repair as needed. If the Power Supply LED is on and the color is green, proceed to [CB10]; if [CB10] is ON, troubleshoot Relay [CR100] and repair as needed. If the Power Supply LED is on and the color is red but not flashing, troubleshoot short-circuited wiring and repair as needed. Repeat the functionality test after all repairs.

decision_path.text: Check Speed display on Package Analytics (10.66.225.88:8080)
Check encoder wheels contact/check any flat spot on wheels. Adjust wheel contact/Replace Encoder
If cable and Encoder both replaced and the speed is still not correct, replace the SIM2000

text: Start by checking the Speed display on Package Analytics (10.66.225.88:8080); if the speed is off by less than 30% proceed to clean the encoder wheels; if the displayed speed is correct proceed to recheck operation; if the speed exceeds 30% examine the encoder cable, verify if the cable is damaged, and replace it and test again if confirmed; if the cable is not damaged proceed to check the encoder wheels' contact and inspect for any flat spots, adjusting the wheel contact or replacing the encoder as needed, then confirm if the system is working correctly; if not, replace the cable and test again, and if the speed remains incorrect after these replacements, replace the SIM2000.

decision_path.text: Check Wiring and fuses then retest
With the path to the reflector clear is the yellow light on?
Does the yellow light go off when a package is present?
Verify and correct alignment of sensor to reflector
Teach sensitivity. Press Teach-in button >2s. Both LED indicators illuminate constantly.
Test again. If it still not working correctly, replace the sensor
Check the reflector and the sensor
Does SIM X4 – Input A 1 change state with the sensor?
Verify SIM programming / functionality. Replace if needed

text: Starting with the initial step, check if the green light is on; if it is not, inspect the wiring and fuses before retesting the system. If the green light is on, verify whether the yellow light is on with the path to the reflector clear; if the yellow light is not on, check the reflector and sensor, ensure proper alignment, press the teach-in button for more than two seconds to illuminate both LED indicators, and if the system still does not function correctly, replace the sensor. If the yellow light is on, assess whether it turns off when a package is present; if it does not, adjust the sensor's alignment to the reflector, activate the sensor's sensitivity by pressing the teach-in button for more than two seconds until both LEDs remain illuminated, and retest the system, replacing the sensor if it continues to malfunction. If the yellow light turns off when a package is present, confirm if SIM X4 – Input A 1 changes state with the sensor; if it does not, troubleshoot and repair the wiring, and if it does, verify the SIM programming and functionality, replacing it if necessary.
